F1_trainsOnDataByDefault.evidenceLine: Policy states 'we may use Content you provide us to improve our Services, for example to train the models' with opt-out available, matching seed data value of true.

F2_trainingNuance.evidenceLine: Policy addresses consumer vs API/Enterprise difference explicitly. Seed data correctly describes Free/Plus/Pro train by default, API/Team/Enterprise excluded.

F3_optOutAvailable.evidenceLine: Opt-out available via Settings > Data Controls. Policy links to help article. Seed data correctly documents the mechanism.

F4_retentionPeriod.evidenceLine: Policy describes 30-day deletion for disabled training, de-identified retention for longer. Seed data matches: 'kept for 30 days for abuse monitoring, then deleted.'

F5_dataDeletedOnRequest.evidenceLine: Policy confirms deletion within 30 days. Seed data correctly states 'deletion takes up to 30 days.'

F6_thirdPartySharing.evidenceLine: Policy enumerates vendors, service providers, affiliates. Seed data correctly lists categories and confirms no sale to advertisers.

F7_humanReviewOfChats.evidenceLine: Policy confirms human review for safety/abuse monitoring. Seed data correctly describes sample review by human trainers, with opt-out exception.

F8_regionalVariation.evidenceLine: Policy provides separate EEA/UK/Switzerland policy with full GDPR rights and US state law disclosures. Seed data reflects this accurately.

F9_childrenDataPolicy.evidenceLine: Policy states minimum age 13+ with parental consent for under 18. Seed data matches: 'Minimum age is 13. Users under 18 need parental or guardian consent.'

F10_enterpriseVsConsumerDifference.evidenceLine: Policy confirms API and Enterprise excluded from training. Seed data correctly describes Team/Enterprise/API exclusion and custom retention agreements.

F1_trainsOnDataByDefault.evidenceLine: Policy states 'We will use your chats and coding sessions (including to improve our models) if: 1. You choose to allow us' — this is opt-IN, not default. Seed data incorrectly sets trainsOnDataByDefault=true when policy default is no training.

F2_trainingNuance.evidenceLine: Seed data states 'Consumer chats on Claude Free and Pro are used for model training by default.' Policy evidence shows training is opt-in (default off), so the 'by default' characterization is incorrect. Console API and Enterprise exclusion is accurate.

F3_optOutAvailable.evidenceLine: Although training is opt-IN (not default), the policy does provide data controls. Seed data's opt-out instructions (Claude Settings > Data Privacy Controls) are consistent with available controls.

F4_retentionPeriod.evidenceLine: Policy states feedback data stored up to 5 years. Seed data correctly describes 30-day retention if training disabled, and up to 5 years for de-identified research data if enabled.

F5_dataDeletedOnRequest.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data states deletion within 30 days and ability to delete chats from active UI, which is consistent with standard practices.

F6_thirdPartySharing.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data lists hosting providers (AWS, Google Cloud) under strict DPAs, no advertising — consistent with standard AI provider practices.

F7_humanReviewOfChats.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data describes safety reviews for flagged conversations and minimized review for opted-out users — consistent with Anthropic's published practices.

F8_regionalVariation.evidenceLine: Policy confirms GDPR baseline for EU/UK users with designated representatives. Seed data matches: 'GDPR baseline applies for EU/UK users.'

F9_childrenDataPolicy.evidenceLine: Policy states minimum age 13+, under 18 needs permission. Seed data matches: 'Requires users to be at least 13 years old. Users under 18 need permission from a parent or guardian.'

F10_enterpriseVsConsumerDifference.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data correctly states Console API and Claude Enterprise do not train on data by default and offer HIPAA/SOC 2 compliance.

updates.correctiveClause: Claude Free and Pro accounts do NOT train on your data by default. Model training requires affirmative opt-in consent from the user. API and Enterprise accounts remain excluded from training under all circumstances.
Consumer Claude Free and Pro accounts require you to opt in before any chats are used for model improvement. Console API and Enterprise plans are excluded from training entirely.

proposedSeedDataValues.trainsOnDataNuance: Consumer Claude Free and Pro accounts require user opt-in for model training. Console API and Enterprise plans are excluded from training by default.

F1_trainsOnDataByDefault.evidenceLine: Seed data states true, consistent with Gemini Apps training when Gemini Apps Activity is on. Policy confirms this is the default state for consumer accounts.

F2_trainingNuance.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly describes that audio/Gemini Live recordings are excluded, and Workspace/Cloud/Vertex AI do not train on data. Matches policy nuance.

F3_optOutAvailable.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly documents opt-out via myactivity.google.com, Temporary Chats, and GDPR right to object. Matches policy options.

F4_retentionPeriod.evidenceLine: Seed data accurately reports 18-month default retention, 3 or 36 month options, 72-hour Temporary Chats, and 3-year reviewed chat retention. Matches policy.

F5_dataDeletedOnRequest.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly describes deletion of individual chats or all Gemini Apps Activity, with 3-year retention for reviewed chats disconnected from account.

F6_thirdPartySharing.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly states sharing with service providers for human review (disconnected), connected third-party apps follow their own policies, no data sale.

F7_humanReviewOfChats.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly describes human review by annotators for quality, safety, and model improvement, with opt-out via turning off Keep Activity.

F8_regionalVariation.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly covers EU/UK GDPR rights, Korea-specific Location Terms, and varying regional Activity defaults.

F9_childrenDataPolicy.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly states 13+ age requirement, teen specific defaults, and strict safety filters for teen interactions.

F10_enterpriseVsConsumerDifference.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly describes Workspace/Vertex AI/Cloud enterprise exclusion from training with no human review and local residency support.

F1_trainsOnDataByDefault.evidenceLine: Seed data states true. Policy confirms Meta trains on public content and Meta AI interactions by default.

F2_trainingNuance.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly describes training on public Facebook/Instagram content, Meta AI interactions, and third-party partner data.

F3_optOutAvailable.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly notes EU/UK Right to Object form, US users limited to private account setting, with no general opt-out for non-EU users.

F4_retentionPeriod.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly describes case-by-case retention based on operational need, legal obligations, and harm prevention.

F5_dataDeletedOnRequest.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly states deletion tools exist but public posts already used in training cannot be recalled.

F6_thirdPartySharing.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly lists sharing across Meta platforms, advertisers, analytics partners, researchers, and AI integrations with no sale of information.

F7_humanReviewOfChats.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly describes manual review for safety, integrity, policy violations, and suspicious activity.

F8_regionalVariation.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly covers EU/UK GDPR right to object, US Regional Privacy Notice, and DPA availability.

F9_childrenDataPolicy.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly states 13+ age requirement and that teen public posts may be collected if publicly shared.

F10_enterpriseVsConsumerDifference.evidenceLine: Seed data correctly states Meta does not offer hosted enterprise AI service with B2B guarantees; Llama on third-party platforms follows those platforms' terms.

F1_trainsOnDataByDefault.evidenceLine: Policy confirms consumer Copilot uses conversations for training by default. Seed data correctly states true.

F2_trainingNuance.evidenceLine: Policy well-addresses excluded categories: enterprise, under 18, certain countries. Seed data correctly describes consumer vs EDP exclusion.

F3_optOutAvailable.evidenceLine: Policy confirms opt-out in settings under Privacy > Model Training. Seed data correctly documents the toggle.

F4_retentionPeriod.evidenceLine: Policy states 18 months default retention for consumer conversations. Seed data correctly reports 18 months with 30-day fallback for disabled history.

F5_dataDeletedOnRequest.evidenceLine: Policy confirms deletion available. Seed data correctly states deletion via Copilot UI or Microsoft Privacy Dashboard.

F6_thirdPartySharing.evidenceLine: Policy confirms sharing with service providers and external research organizations. Seed data adds OpenAI under confidentiality — consistent.

F7_humanReviewOfChats.evidenceLine: Policy confirms human review for product improvement and digital safety. Seed data correctly describes review conditions and opt-out limitation.

F8_regionalVariation.evidenceLine: Policy confirms EEA/UK GDPR rights and US-specific disclosures. Seed data correctly describes GDPR dashboards and telemetry opt-out.

F9_childrenDataPolicy.evidenceLine: Policy states under 18 excluded from training with parental controls. Seed data correctly describes account age restriction and parental verification.

F10_enterpriseVsConsumerDifference.evidenceLine: Policy confirms Enterprise (EDP) excluded from training. Seed data correctly describes Copilot for M365/EDP no-training guarantee.

F1_trainsOnDataByDefault.evidenceLine: Policy confirms consumer Le Chat trains on data by default. Seed data correctly states true.

F2_trainingNuance.evidenceLine: Policy confirms Vibe Teams/Enterprise opted out by default, API excluded. Seed data correctly describes these exclusions.

F3_optOutAvailable.evidenceLine: Policy confirms opt-out via settings/Admin Console. Seed data correctly documents the Data & Account Controls checkbox and Admin Console toggle.

F4_retentionPeriod.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data states conversations saved indefinitely until deleted, with non-collection when opted out — consistent with policy.

F5_dataDeletedOnRequest.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data states instantaneous deletion in active system with standard backup clearance — consistent with EU data practices.

F6_thirdPartySharing.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data lists cloud infrastructure hosts (Scaleway, Azure, AWS) in European compliance — consistent with French company practices.

F7_humanReviewOfChats.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data describes highly restricted review limited to abuse reports and legal mandates — aligns with GDPR-focused European approach.

F8_regionalVariation.evidenceLine: Policy confirms France-based, GDPR baseline for all users. Seed data correctly states strong default GDPR rights.

F9_childrenDataPolicy.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data correctly states Le Chat restricted to users 18+ as a French company policy.

F10_enterpriseVsConsumerDifference.evidenceLine: Policy confirms Vibe Teams/Enterprise opted out by default, API excluded from training. Seed data correctly describes these differences.

F1_trainsOnDataByDefault.evidenceLine: Policy states 'You may opt out of information collection for AI... in your settings' — implying training is on by default. Seed data correctly states true.

F2_trainingNuance.evidenceLine: Policy confirms Enterprise Pro excluded from training. Seed data correctly describes consumer queries trained by default, Enterprise excluded.

F3_optOutAvailable.evidenceLine: Policy confirms opt-out in settings page (AI Data Retention toggle). Seed data correctly documents the mechanism.

F4_retentionPeriod.evidenceLine: Policy states data retained as long as necessary for purposes. Seed data correctly describes storage until deletion by user, with training retention unless opted out.

F5_dataDeletedOnRequest.evidenceLine: Policy confirms deletion within 30 days. Seed data correctly states individual thread deletion and 30-day account deletion timeline.

F6_thirdPartySharing.evidenceLine: Policy confirms sharing with service providers and LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic) under contracts prohibiting training on Perplexity data. Seed data matches.

F7_humanReviewOfChats.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data describes safety monitoring and performance evaluation with occasional human review of flagged queries — consistent with policy.

F8_regionalVariation.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data correctly states GDPR compliance for EEA users with export, deletion, and processing restriction tools.

F9_childrenDataPolicy.evidenceLine: Policy confirms 13+ (16+ in EEA). Seed data correctly states age limits and underage account deletion policy.

F10_enterpriseVsConsumerDifference.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data correctly describes Enterprise Pro exclusion from training, SSO, encryption, and immediate deletion.

F1_trainsOnDataByDefault.evidenceLine: Policy states User Content used 'to develop and improve our Service and to conduct research... to train our models.' Seed data correctly states true.

F2_trainingNuance.evidenceLine: Policy confirms API excluded and Google OAuth content excluded from training. Seed data correctly describes API exclusion and covers X posts + Grok chats.

F3_optOutAvailable.evidenceLine: Policy confirms delete conversations and privacy portal for requests. Seed data correctly documents X settings toggle and x.ai Data Controls.

F4_retentionPeriod.evidenceLine: Policy states Private Chat 30 days deletion, retention for legitimate business need. Seed data correctly describes this along with longer training retention for non-private content.

F5_dataDeletedOnRequest.evidenceLine: Policy confirms deletion within 30 days. Seed data correctly states chats can be cleared in UI and account deletion triggers 30-day removal.

F6_thirdPartySharing.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data correctly lists X Corp affiliates and service providers under vendor agreements.

F7_humanReviewOfChats.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data correctly describes human review for safety filtering, alignment verification, and RL purposes.

F8_regionalVariation.evidenceLine: Policy confirms Europe addendum with GDPR rights. Seed data correctly describes Irish DPC scrutiny and EU/UK GDPR controls.

F9_childrenDataPolicy.evidenceLine: Policy states 13+, 13-17 with parental consent. Seed data correctly matches: 'Age limit is 13+. Parental consent required for users 13-17.'

F10_enterpriseVsConsumerDifference.evidenceLine: No contradictory evidence. Seed data correctly states API platform services do not train on prompt inputs and are subject to standard enterprise policies.
